_meta.description: Effective tax rates by wealth/income class
sources: Saez & Zucman (2019), The Triumph of Injustice
EU Tax Observatory, Global Tax Evasion Report 2024
NZ Inland Revenue, High-Wealth Individuals Research Project (April 2023)
Revenue Commissioners Ireland, Income Tax Distribution Tables
Statistik Austria, Integrated Wage and Income Tax Statistics
SII Chile & WID.world Chile distributional accounts
_meta.methodology: Effective rate = total taxes paid (income, payroll, corporate, property, estate, consumption) / total pre-tax economic income

US.source: Saez & Zucman (2019), The Triumph of Injustice; updated with IRS microdata through 2018

GB.source: Advani, Chamberlain & Summers (2023); HMRC Survey of Personal Incomes; ONS household data

FR.source: Landais, Saez & Zucman (2020); WID.world France series; EU Tax Observatory (2024)

DE.source: Bach, Beznoska & Steiner (2020), DIW Berlin; Bundesfinanzministerium Datensammlung; OECD 2024

NL.source: CPB Netherlands Bureau; CBS income statistics; EU Tax Observatory (2024)

SE.source: Waldenström (2020), IFN Stockholm; SCB tax statistics; WID.world Sweden series

NO.source: Alstadsæter, Johannesen & Zucman (2019); SSB tax statistics; WID.world Norway series

DK.source: Danish Ministry of Taxation; OECD Tax Database 2024; WID.world Denmark series

CH.source: Swiss Federal Tax Administration; Brülhart et al. (2022); OECD Tax Database 2024

JP.source: National Tax Agency statistics; Moriguchi & Saez (2008, updated); OECD Tax Database 2024

CA.source: Statistics Canada; PBO Distributional Analysis 2023; OECD Tax Database 2024

AU.source: ATO Taxation Statistics; Leigh (2009, updated); Grattan Institute distributional analysis

IT.source: Ministero dell'Economia; Acciari & Morelli (2023); EU Tax Observatory (2024)

ES.source: Agencia Tributaria; Alvaredo & Saez (2009, updated); EU Tax Observatory (2024)

BR.source: Receita Federal; Morgan (2017), WID.world; Gobetti & Orair (2017)

ZA.source: SARS Tax Statistics; Chatterjee, Czajka & Gethin (2022), WID.world; National Treasury

NZ.source: NZ Inland Revenue, High-Wealth Individuals Research Project (April 2023): median effective rate of 8.9% for 311 high-wealth individuals on economic income incl. unrealised gains, vs 20.2% for middle NZ. Lower brackets from NZ Treasury distributional analysis. No general capital gains tax.

FI.source: Statistics Finland income distribution statistics; Verohallinto public tax data. Finland's dual income tax system: progressive earned income tax up to ~51.4%, flat 30–34% capital income tax. Sub-percentile rates estimated from capital vs labour income shares. OECD Taxing Wages 2025: average net tax rate 30.3%.

CL.source: SII (Servicio de Impuestos Internos) bracket data; WID.world Chile distributional accounts; López & Figueroa (Harvard CID). Highly regressive due to 19% IVA consumption tax burden on lower incomes and favourable capital income treatment. Top 1% earn ~25% of pre-tax income (WID). Sub-percentile effective rates estimated.

BE.source: Statbel fiscal income statistics (decile distribution of net taxable income, total tax, and average tax rate, income year 2023). Very high social contributions (~13% employee). No general capital gains tax; 30% withholding on dividends. Sub-percentile rates estimated from OECD/WID capital income concentration.

IE.source: Revenue Commissioners income tax distribution tables; CSO Ireland's Tax Statistics 2024; Social Justice Ireland effective rate analysis (10.3% at €25K, 39.0% at €120K). Top rate = 40% income tax + 8% USC. Top 1% (>€203K) pay ~19% of personal tax. Entrepreneur relief (10% CGT) benefits top brackets.

AT.source: Statistik Austria, Integrierte Lohn- und Einkommensteuerstatistik (integrated wage and income tax statistics). Social contributions ~18% employee share, capped. Flat 27.5% KESt on capital income reduces effective rates for top brackets. Sub-percentile rates estimated from capital income concentration data.
